error_message: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open "attempts/1/execution-recovery.json"
root_cause: AttemptArtifacts did not expose controller-owned execution recovery metadata, so writeAttemptArtifacts never persisted execution-recovery.json.
fix: Added the ExecutionRecovery runtime type, extended AttemptArtifacts with executionRecovery, persisted attempts/<n>/execution-recovery.json, and added a regression test.

root_cause: The task brief used a Jest-style flag, but this repository runs Vitest and does not support --runInBand.
fix: Ran the focused test with the Vitest-compatible command `npm test -- tests/persistence/fileStore.test.ts` instead of using the unsupported flag.

root_cause: The isolated d-scenario-boundary-classification worktree has a placeholder node_modules directory with no local .bin/tsx binary, so validation evidence tests that spawn the worktree-local tsx executable fail before exercising product code.
fix: Not fixed in Task 1; recorded as a pre-existing environment issue after the required full-suite run.

error_message: Transform failed with ERROR: Unterminated string literal in tests/controller/runLoop.integration.test.ts
root_cause: A Python-generated test edit wrote a physical newline into a TypeScript string literal instead of an escaped \n sequence, so Vitest/esbuild could not parse the file.
fix: Replaced the broken multiline literal with an escaped newline string before re-running the focused Task 2 integration tests.

error_message: execute_started missing and execution-recovery.json not written when execute timed out/exhausted without a result
root_cause: The controller entered the execute phase without emitting a dedicated execute boundary event, and the execute timeout path that returned null only persisted plan artifacts instead of a controller-owned recovery observation before cleanup.
fix: Emitted execute_started immediately before adapter.execute, captured changed paths from a best-effort git status probe before cleanup, persisted execution-recovery.json for interrupted execute null-result exhaustion paths, and updated integration coverage.

error_message: execute-entered abort-throw paths could end failed/attempt_failed without execution-recovery.json, and execution-recovery.json could claim cleanupStatus="removed" before cleanup actually ran
root_cause: runPhaseWithTimeout rethrew post-abort execute errors as PhaseExecutionError, so the controller's interrupted-execute null-result recovery branch only covered abort-return-null. The controller also serialized execution-recovery.json before cleanup and hard-coded cleanupStatus to removed.
fix: Captured timed-out aborted errors as no-result timeout outcomes, persisted interrupted execute recovery for abort-throw as well as abort-return-null, and finalized execution-recovery.json cleanupStatus from the actual cleanup result (retained on cleanup failure, removed after successful cleanup).

error_message: TypeError: classifyDScenarioBoundary is not a function in tests/validation/evidence.test.ts
root_cause: Task 3 tests started importing D-boundary helpers before evidence.ts exported them or exposed parsed event types needed for deterministic classification.
fix: Added DBoundaryClassification exports, classifyDScenarioBoundary/mapDBoundaryToReview helpers, and event type parsing on evidence observations.

error_message: Task 3 mapping test mis-modeled recoverable execute evidence and failed after the classifier required execution-recovery.json or equivalent controller-owned evidence
root_cause: The test expected EXECUTE_ENTERED_WITH_RECOVERABLE_EVIDENCE from execute_started plus standard artifacts alone, but the approved D-boundary spec requires controller-owned recoverable evidence such as execution-recovery.json when no complete execute result is available.
fix: Updated the failing test to write attempts/1/execution-recovery.json before collecting evidence, so the fixture actually exercises the recoverable-evidence branch.

error_message: Malformed or shape-invalid execution-recovery.json was treated as sufficient recoverable evidence in Task 3 D-boundary classification
root_cause: collectEvidence treated any JSON-parsed execution-recovery.json as PRESENT without validating the ExecutionRecovery schema, so classifyDScenarioBoundary could trust malformed Layer A recovery evidence.
fix: Schema-validated execution-recovery.json, surfaced shape-invalid recovery as INVALID, forced invalid recovery evidence to map to BOUNDARY_UNRESOLVED, and added a focused regression test.

error_message: Historical PRE_EXECUTE_EXHAUSTION classification ignored verify.json and could misclassify runs where later attempt handling had already begun
root_cause: matchesPreExecuteExhaustion looked only at plan/execution/diff/log plus events, so D evidence with verify.json present could still slip into the historical pre-execute branch even though verify output is Layer A proof of later handling.
fix: Required verify to remain NOT_RUN for PRE_EXECUTE_EXHAUSTION and added a regression that verify.json presence forces BOUNDARY_UNRESOLVED for historical D evidence.

error_message: Transform failed with ERROR: Unterminated string literal / Unexpected ")" in tests/validation/evidence.test.ts during Task 3 reviewer fix wave 2
root_cause: A Python-written regression test inserted a physical newline into a TypeScript string literal and then a cleanup edit left an extra closing parenthesis, so esbuild failed before test collection.
fix: Repaired the generated `"\n"` literal and removed the stray `)` in the new execution-recovery regression, then reran focused and full Vitest suites successfully.

error_message: finalize-review could only write review.json and refused historical D reclassification once review.json already existed
root_cause: The CLI only supported creating review.json and had no separate reclassification artifact schema or arguments, so historical review preservation from the D-boundary spec was impossible.
fix: Added ReclassifiedReview validation, a separate review-reclassified.json write path behind explicit reclassification arguments, and regression coverage that preserves the original review.json while recording original/reclassified verdicts, boundary classification, rule version, and Layer A evidence references.

error_message: Scenario D contradiction detection missed raw Layer A execute/diff/log files when scenario normalization downgraded them to INVALID
root_cause: hasContradictoryLayerAEvidence() relied on scenario-normalized artifact statuses, so physically present Layer A artifacts that normalized to INVALID did not count as contradiction evidence.
fix: Added raw Layer A observation checks for execution.json, diff.patch, and stdout-stderr.log so contradictory presence now maps to BOUNDARY_UNRESOLVED even when Scenario D marks the artifact INVALID.

error_message: parseChangedPathsFromGitStatus() misparsed porcelain -z rename/copy records and embedded a literal NUL byte in source
root_cause: The helper treated rename/copy records as if the second token were the destination path and used a physical NUL character in the source literal instead of an escaped delimiter.
fix: Switched to split("\0"), kept the current record path as the rename/copy destination, skipped the following source-path token, and added a direct regression test.

error_message: Scenario D contradiction detection ignored controller-owned execution-recovery.json presence and could misclassify contradictory event chains as EXECUTE_ENTERED_NO_RECOVERABLE_EVIDENCE
root_cause: hasContradictoryLayerAEvidence() only treated raw execution/diff/log artifacts as Layer A contradiction evidence, so valid execution-recovery.json could coexist with missing attempt/execute boundary events without forcing BOUNDARY_UNRESOLVED.
fix: Counted controller-owned execution-recovery.json presence as Layer A boundary evidence in the contradiction matrix, added focused regressions for missing attempt_started and missing execute_started event chains, and documented the operator reclassification flow.

error_message: validation/v1/lib/evidence.ts still hard-coded Scenario D attempt artifact reads to attempts/1, so exhausted multi-attempt runs ignored terminal-attempt Layer A evidence under attempts/2+
root_cause: The evidence layer derived cleanup from loop-state attempt context but continued to build plan/execution/verify/diff/log and execution-recovery paths with a literal attempts/1, so multi-attempt exhausted D runs were classified against the wrong attempt.
fix: Resolved the terminal attempt from loop-state currentAttempt/attemptsUsed before attempt-scoped reads, routed all Layer A attempt artifact paths through a shared helper, and added an exhausted attempt-2 regression covering attempts/2/execution-recovery.json.

error_message: docs/handover/ccloop-handover.md contained duplicated takeover sections and stale repository facts
root_cause: The handover accumulated historical updates without a full current-truth rewrite, so duplicate sections and outdated HEAD/state statements remained in the operator-facing takeover document.
fix: Rewrote the handover as a single-copy current-truth document aligned to the verified main HEAD, accepted A/B/C/D/E review artifacts, preserved evidence boundaries, and current validation README guidance.
